name: Purple Team Home Lab — Detection Coverage
description: Detection coverage for the Purple Team Home Lab (MARVEL.LOCAL AD). 13 techniques across an 8-phase kill chain, each paired with a Splunk detection and Sigma rule. Green = detected with production-grade rule. Yellow = attack attempted but blocked/failed — documented in lab_issues.md. Repo: github.com/slim92code/purple-team-lab

techniques.comment: Detected — nmap network sweep + LDAP/Kerberos user enumeration. Sysmon EC3 + Security EC4625. Detection Card #1.
Detected — AD account enumeration via kerbrute. Detection Card #1.
Password spraying detected. See sub-technique T1110.003.
Detected — kerbrute password spray. EC4625 across many accounts from single source + EC4740 lockout on pparker. Detection Card #2.

metadata.name: Detection
metadata.value: Splunk: distinct target accounts per source IP over time window

techniques.comment: LOLBin execution detected. See sub-technique T1218.004.
Detected — InstallUtil.exe LOLBin execution from non-standard path. Sysmon EC1 process creation. Detection Card #3.

metadata.name: Detection
metadata.value: Splunk: InstallUtil.exe spawning from non-standard path

techniques.comment: Detected — Kerberoasting via GetUserSPNs. Security EC4769 with RC4 (0x17) ticket encryption for SQLService SPN. Central segment (Seg 4). Detection Card #4.
Credential dumping — multiple sub-techniques exercised. See sub-techniques.
Detected — SAM dump via Impacket secretsdump through Sliver shell using RemoteRegistry. Detection Card #6.

metadata.name: Detection
metadata.value: Splunk: RemoteRegistry access + secretsdump behavioral pattern

techniques.comment: Detected — cached domain credentials (SECURITY hive). Detection Card #6.

GAP — DCSync attempt via SharpKatz FAILED. AMSI blocked Mimikatz; SharpKatz hit RPC error 1825 (Kerberos auth failure at DRS bind transport layer). Attempt telemetry captured by Sysmon EC1 but no successful replication. Blue-team win. See lab_issues.md.

metadata.name: Outcome
metadata.value: Attack failed — AMSI + RPC 1825 (transport-layer auth failure, not permissions)

techniques.comment: Scheduled task persistence. See sub-technique T1053.005.
Detected — Scheduled Task creation. Security EC4698 + Sysmon EC1. Detection Card #7.

metadata.name: Detection
metadata.value: Splunk: EC4698 task creation with suspicious action path

techniques.comment: C2 communication over web protocols. See sub-technique T1071.001 — the showcase detection.

SHOWCASE — Sliver C2 beacon over mTLS (port 443) from C:\Temp. Default SwiftOnSecurity Sysmon config MISSED the NetworkConnect from C:\Temp; closed with a single behavioral rule (location-based, not filename-based). Detection Cards #5 and #8. Climax finding (Seg 8).

metadata.name: Detection
metadata.value: Splunk: Sysmon EC3 NetworkConnect from C:\Temp watchlist path

metadata.name: Gap to Fix
metadata.value: Default config blind to C:\Temp beacon — fixed with behavioral rule on process location

techniques.comment: Detected — protocol tunneling via Sliver mTLS listener. Detection Card #5.
Detected — exfiltration over the established Sliver C2 channel. Detection Card #8.

metadata.name: Detection
metadata.value: Splunk: outbound data over established C2 beacon

legendItems.label: Attempted but blocked/failed — documented in lab_issues.md (score 50)

metadata.name: Detections
metadata.value: 9 production Sigma/Splunk saved searches; 30+ total SPL queries

metadata.name: Blue-team wins
metadata.value: AMSI blocked Mimikatz DCSync; AppLocker blocked C2 implant from C:\Temp
